name: Medtronic Twin Cities Marathon 2026
notes: Point-to-point. Fast first 20 miles disguise the Summit Avenue climb. The hill at miles 20-23 is race-defining. Mile 13.5 Minnehaha Parkway rise shocks runners who haven't studied the course. The downhill at mile 18 should be used for recovery, not speed.

elevation_profile.segment_note: Chain of lakes — flat, deceptively fast
TRAP: Minnehaha Parkway — subtle but real, shocks everyone
Lovely downhill — USE FOR RECOVERY NOT SPEED
Bridge crossing — do not surge here
RACE STARTS HERE — first Summit hill
Summit Ave climb — shorten stride, run effort
Summit continues — do not look for the top
Final summit pitch — hardest on tired legs
Cathedral of Saint Paul — crowd roars

segments.name: Downtown and early hills
segments.strategy: Run 15 sec/mi slower than goal pace. The downhill feel is deceptive. Bank energy, not time.

segments.name: Chain of Lakes
segments.strategy: Lock into goal pace. HR should be comfortably controlled. Resist the urge to go faster.

segments.name: Minnehaha trap
segments.strategy: This rise shocks everyone. Run by effort not pace. Do not try to maintain pace here. Trap for the unprepared.

segments.name: Mississippi banks
segments.strategy: Crowd thins. Stay patient. The mile 18 downhill is a recovery opportunity — do not surge.

segments.name: Summit Avenue climb — the race
segments.strategy: Look at the mansions, not the top of the hill. Shorten stride. Everyone slows here. The runner who slows least wins. Race starts here.

segments.name: Capitol descent
segments.strategy: Recover at 23-23.5. Then from 23.5, race to the finish. You have been holding back for 20 miles.
